messages.name: MacOSDeploymentTargetConflict
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message:
Conflicting specification for minimum macOS deployment target!
If your conda_build_config.yaml sets `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET`, please change the name of that key to `c_stdlib_version`!
Continuing with `max(c_stdlib_version, MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET)`.

messages.name: MacOSDeploymentTargetBelowStdlib
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message:
You are setting `MACOSX_SDK_VERSION` below `c_stdlib_version`, in conda_build_config.yaml which is not possible! Please ensure `MACOSX_SDK_VERSION` is at least `c_stdlib_version` (you can leave it out if it is equal).
If you are not setting `c_stdlib_version` yourself, this means you are requesting a version below the current global baseline in conda-forge (${baseline}). If this is the intention, you also need to override `c_stdlib_version` and `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET` locally.

messages.name: MaintainerMissing
messages.documentation:
Maintainers listed in `extra.recipe-maintainers` must be valid Github usernames
or `@conda-forge/*` teams.
messages.message: Recipe maintainer ${team_or}"${maintainer}" does not exist
examples: Recipe maintainer team "@conda-forge/deleted-team" does not exist

messages.name: PackageToAvoid
messages.documentation:
Some package names may not be used in recipes directly, or under some circumstances.

The full list of package names and their explanations can be found in
[`conda-forge-pinning-feedstock`](https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge-pinning-feedstock/blob/main/recipe/linter_hints/hints.toml).
messages.message: ${package_hint}

messages.name: NoVariantConfigs
messages.documentation:
No variant config files could be found in `.ci_support/*.yaml` , which means that
build matrix is empty and no packages will be built.

This is usually caused by a misconfiguration of your recipe file (e.g. `build.skip` is always
`true`, disabling all builds).
messages.message: The feedstock has no `.ci_support` files and thus will not build any packages.

messages.name: NoEmptyVariantsFile
messages.documentation: Variants files can't be empty.
messages.message: The recipe should not have an empty `conda_build_config.yaml` file.

messages.name: NoCustomGHAWorkflows
messages.documentation:
Due to its stature in the open-source community, conda-forge has enhanced
access to certain CI services. This access is a community resource entrusted
to conda-forge for use in building packages. We thus cannot support
third-party or "off-label" CI jobs in our feedstocks on any of our CI
services. If we find such use, we will politely ask the maintainers to
rectify the situation. We may take more serious actions, including archiving
feedstocks or removing maintainers from the organization, if the situation
cannot be rectified.
messages.message: conda-forge feedstocks cannot have custom Github Actions workflows. See https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2750 for more information. If you didn't add any custom workflows, please consider rerendering your feedstock to remove deprecated workflows.

messages.name: NoDuplicateKeys
messages.documentation: `conda-forge.yml` must not contain duplicate keys.
messages.message: The ``conda-forge.yml`` file is not allowed to have duplicate keys.

messages.name: UnexpectedSection
messages.documentation:
Recipe files must not contain unknown top-level keys.

For recipe version 0, the allowed keys are (in this order):

- `package`
- `source`
- `build`
- `requirements`
- `test`
- `app`
- `outputs`
- `about`
- `extra`

For recipe version 1, it depends if you are generating one or
multiple artifacts. For single artifacts, the expected keys are
(in this order):

- `schema_version`
- `context`
- `package`
- `source`
- `build`
- `requirements`
- `tests`
- `about`
- `extra`

For multiple artifacts, the expected keys are (in this order):

- `schema_version`
- `context`
- `recipe`
- `source`
- `build`
- `outputs`
- `about`
- `extra`
messages.message: The top level meta key ${section} is unexpected

messages.name: SectionOrder
messages.documentation:
The top-level sections of a recipe file must always follow the same order.

Please refer to linter rule [`R-000`](#R-000) (`RecipeUnexpectedSection`) for more
details.
messages.message: The top level meta keys are in an unexpected order. Expecting ${order}.

messages.name: MissingAboutItem
messages.documentation: The `about` section requires three fields: homepage (`home` in v1), license, and summary.
messages.message: The ${item} item is expected in the about section.

messages.name: NoMaintainers
messages.documentation: All recipes must list at least one maintainer under `extra/recipe-maintainers`.
messages.message: The recipe could do with some maintainers listed in the `extra/recipe-maintainers` section.

messages.name: MaintainersMustBeList
messages.documentation: The `extra/recipe-maintainers` only accepts a list of strings as a value.
messages.message: Recipe maintainers should be a json list.

messages.name: RequiredTests
messages.documentation: All recipes must have a non-empty `test` section.
messages.message: The recipe must have some tests.

messages.name: RecommendedTests
messages.documentation: All recipes must have a non-empty `test` section.
messages.message: It looks like the '${output}' output doesn't have any tests.

messages.name: UnknownLicense
messages.documentation: All recipes must have a license identifier, but it can't be "unknown".
messages.message: The recipe license cannot be unknown.

messages.name: BuildNumberMissing
messages.documentation: All recipes must define a `build.number` value.
messages.message: The recipe must have a `build/number` section.

messages.name: RequirementsOrder
messages.documentation:
The different subcategories of the `requirements` section must follow
a strict order: `build`, `host`, `run`, `run_constrained`.
messages.message: The `requirements/` sections should be defined in the following order: ${expected}; instead saw: ${seen}.

messages.name: LicenseFieldMentionsLicense
messages.documentation: Licenses should omit the term 'License' in its name.
messages.message: The recipe `license` should not include the word "License".

messages.name: TooManyEmptyLines
messages.documentation: Recipe files should end with a single empty line, not more.
messages.message: There are ${n_lines} too many lines.  There should be one empty line at the end of the file.

messages.name: TooFewEmptyLines
messages.documentation: Recipe files should end with a single empty line.
messages.message: There are too few lines.  There should be one empty line at the end of the file.

messages.name: LicenseFamily
messages.documentation: The field `license_file` must be always present.
messages.message: license_file entry is missing, but is required.

messages.name: InvalidPackageName
messages.documentation:
The recipe `name` can only contain certain characters:

- lowercase ASCII letters (`a-z`)
- digits (`0-9`)
- underscores, hyphens and dots (`_`, `-`, `.`)
messages.message: Recipe name has invalid characters. only lowercase alpha, numeric, underscores, hyphens and dots allowed

messages.name: MissingVersion
messages.documentation: The package `version` field is required.
messages.message: Package version is missing.

messages.name: InvalidVersion
messages.documentation: The package `version` field must be a valid version string.
messages.message: Package version ${version} doesn't match conda spec: ${error}

messages.name: PinnedNumpy
messages.documentation: See <https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#linking-numpy>
messages.message: Using pinned numpy packages is a deprecated pattern. Consider using the method outlined [conda-forge.org > Docs > Maintainer Documentation > Knowledge Base > Building Against NumPy](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#linking-numpy).

messages.name: UnexpectedSubsection
messages.documentation:
This check ensures that the passed recipe conforms to the expected recipe v0 schema.

See schema in [`conda_build.metadata.FIELDS`](
https://github.com/conda/conda-build/blob/25.9.0/conda_build/metadata.py#L619)
messages.message: The ${section} section contained an unexpected subsection name. ${subsection} is not a valid subsection name.

messages.name: SourceHash
messages.documentation: All recipe source URLs must have a hash checksum for integrity checks.
messages.message: When defining a source/url please add a sha256, sha1 or md5 checksum (sha256 preferably).

messages.name: NoarchValue
messages.documentation: The `build.noarch` field can only take `python` or `generic` as a value.
messages.message: Invalid `noarch` value `${given}`. Should be one of `${valid}`.

messages.name: RequirementJoinVersionOperator
messages.documentation:
conda recipes should use the three-field matchspec syntax to express requirements:
`name [version [build]]`. This means having no spaces between operator and version
literals.
messages.message: ``requirements: ${section}: ${requirement}`` should not contain a space between relational operator and the version, i.e. ``${name} ${pin}``

messages.name: RequirementSeparateNameVersion
messages.documentation:
conda recipes should use the three-field matchspec syntax to express requirements:
`name [version [build]]`. This means having a space between name and version.
messages.message: ``requirements: ${section}: ${requirement}`` must contain a space between the name and the pin, i.e. ``${name} ${pin}``

messages.name: LanguageHostRun
messages.documentation:
Packages may depend on certain languages (e.g. Python, R) that require depending
on the language runtime both in `host` and `run`.
messages.message: If ${language} is a host requirement, it should be a run requirement.

messages.name: LanguageHostRunUnpinned
messages.documentation:
Packages may depend on certain languages (e.g. Python, R) that require depending
on the language runtime both in `host` and `run`. They should not pin it to a
particular version when the package is not `noarch`.
messages.message: Non noarch packages should have ${language} requirement without any version constraints.

messages.name: JinjaExpression
messages.documentation: Jinja expressions should add a space between the double curly braces.
messages.message: Jinja2 variable references are suggested to take a ``${dollar}{{<one space><variable name><one space>}}`` form. See lines ${lines}.

messages.name: PythonLowerBound
messages.documentation: Noarch Python recipes should always pin the lower bound on their `python` requirement.
messages.message: noarch: python recipes are required to have a lower bound on the python version. Typically this means putting `python >={{ python_min }}` in the `run` section of your recipe. You may also want to check the upstream source for the package's Python compatibility.

messages.name: PinSubpackagePinCompatible
messages.documentation:
The Jinja functions `pin_subpackage` and `pin_compatible` may be confused
because both would add version constraints to a package name. However, they
have different purposes.

- `pin_subpackage()` must be used when the package to be pinned is a known output
  in the current recipe.
- `pin_compatible()` must be used when the package to be pinned is _not_ an output
  of the current recipe.
messages.message: ${should_use} should be used instead of ${in_use} for `${pin}` because it is ${what} known outputs of this recipe: ${subpackages}.

messages.name: CompiledWheelsNotAllowed
messages.documentation:
Python wheels are often discouraged as package sources. This is especially the case
for compiled wheels, which are forbidden.
messages.message: Detected compiled wheel(s) in source: ${urls}. This is disallowed. All packages should be built from source except in rare and exceptional cases.

messages.name: PureWheelsNotAllowed
messages.documentation:
Python wheels are often discouraged as package sources. This is also the case
for pure Python wheels when building non-noarch packages.
messages.message: Detected pure Python wheel(s) in source: ${urls}. This is discouraged. Please consider using a source distribution (sdist) instead.

messages.name: PureWheelsNotAllowedNoarch
messages.documentation:
Python wheels are often discouraged as package sources. However, pure Python
wheels may be used as a source for noarch Python packages, although sdists are preferred.
messages.message: Detected pure Python wheel(s) in source: ${urls}. This is generally ok for pure Python wheels and noarch=python packages but it's preferred to use a source distribution (sdist) if possible.

messages.name: RustLicenses
messages.documentation: <https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs/#rust>
messages.message: Rust packages must include the licenses of the Rust dependencies. For more info, visit: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs/#rust

messages.name: GoLicenses
messages.documentation: <https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs/#go>
messages.message: Go packages must include the licenses of the Go dependencies. For more info, visit: https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs/#go

messages.name: StdlibJinja
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: This recipe is using a compiler, which now requires adding a build dependence on `${dollar}{{ stdlib("c") }}` as well. Note that this rule applies to each output of the recipe using a compiler. For further details, please see https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102.

messages.name: StdlibSysroot
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: You're setting a requirement on sysroot_linux-<arch> directly; this should now be done by adding a build dependence on `${dollar}{{ stdlib("c") }}`, and overriding `c_stdlib_version` in `recipe/conda_build_config.yaml` for the respective platform as necessary. For further details, please see https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102.

messages.name: StdlibMacOS
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: You're setting a constraint on the `__osx` virtual package directly; this should now be done by adding a build dependence on `${dollar}{{ stdlib("c") }}`, and overriding `c_stdlib_version` in `recipe/conda_build_config.yaml` for the respective platform as necessary. For further details, please see https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base/#requiring-newer-macos-sdks.

messages.name: NotParsableLint
messages.documentation:
The conda recipe should be parsable by at least one backend.
If none can parse it, this constitutes an error that needs to be remediated.
messages.message: The recipe is not parsable by any of the known recipe parsers (${parsers}). Please check the logs for more information and ensure your recipe can be parsed.

messages.name: NotParsableHint
messages.documentation:
The conda recipe should be parsable by at least one backend.
Sometimes, only some backends fail, which is not critical, but should be looked into.
messages.message: (dynamic)
examples: The recipe is not parsable by parser `conda-recipe-manager`. The recipe can only be automatically migrated to the new v1 format if it is parseable by conda-recipe-manager.

The recipe is not parsable by parser `other`. Your recipe may not receive automatic updates and/or may not be compatible with conda-forge's infrastructure. Please check the logs for more information and ensure your recipe can be parsed.

messages.name: PythonIsAbi3Bool
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: The `is_abi3` variant variable is now a boolean value instead of a string (i.e., 'true' or 'false'). Please change syntax like `is_abi3 == 'true' to `is_abi3`.

messages.name: ExtraFeedstockNameSuffix
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: The feedstock-name in the `extra` section must not end with '-feedstock'. The '-feedstock' suffix is automatically appended during feedstock creation.

messages.name: VersionParsedAsFloat
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: ${key} has a value that is interpreted as a floating-point number. Please quote it (like `"${value}"`${v0_hint}) to ensure that it is interpreted as string and preserved exactly.

messages.name: SuggestNoarch
messages.documentation:
`noarch` packages are strongly preferred when possible.
See https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#noarch-builds.
messages.message: Whenever possible python packages should use noarch. See https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#noarch-builds

messages.name: ScriptShellcheckReport
messages.documentation:
This issue is raised when `shellcheck` is enabled and detects problems
in your build `.sh` scripts.

See https://www.shellcheck.net/wiki/ for details on the shellcheck error codes.
messages.message: (dynamic)
examples:
Whenever possible fix all shellcheck findings ('shellcheck --enable=all --shell=bash --exclude=SC2154 recipe/*.sh -f diff | git apply' helps)

```text
In ./recipe/build.sh line 337:
ln -sf $PREFIX/$f $PWD/$f
        ^-----^ SC2086 (info): Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.
                ^-- SC2086 (info): Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.
                ^--^ SC2086 (info): Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.
                        ^-- SC2086 (info): Double quote to prevent globbing and word splitting.

Did you mean:
          ln -sf "$PREFIX"/"$f" "$PWD"/"$f"
```

messages.name: ScriptShellcheckFailure
messages.documentation:
This issue is raised when `shellcheck` is enabled but could not
run successfully (something went wrong).
messages.message: There have been errors while scanning with shellcheck.

messages.name: LicenseSPDX
messages.documentation:
The `license` field must be a valid SPDX identifier.

See list at [`licenses.txt`](https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-smithy/blob/main/conda_smithy/linter/licenses.txt).
messages.message:
License is not an SPDX identifier (or a custom LicenseRef) nor an SPDX license expression.

Documentation on acceptable licenses can be found [conda-forge.org > Docs > Maintainer Documentation > Contributing packages > SPDX Identifiers and Expressions](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#spdx-identifiers-and-expressions).

messages.name: InvalidLicenseException
messages.documentation:
The `license` field may accept some SPDX exception expressions, as controlled
in [this file](https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-smithy/blob/main/conda_smithy/linter/license_exceptions.txt)
messages.message:
License exception is not an SPDX exception.

Documentation on acceptable licenses can be found [conda-forge.org > Docs > Maintainer Documentation > Contributing packages > SPDX Identifiers and Expressions](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#spdx-identifiers-and-expressions).

messages.name: PythonBuildBackendHost
messages.documentation: Build backends in Python packages must be explictly added to `host`.
messages.message: No valid build backend found for Python recipe for package `${package_name}` using `pip`. Python recipes using `pip` need to explicitly specify a build backend in the `host` section. If your recipe has built with only `pip` in the `host` section in the past, you likely should add `setuptools` to the `host` section of your recipe.

messages.name: PythonMinPin
messages.documentation:
Python packages should depend on certain `>={min_version}` at runtime,
but build and test against `{min_version}.*`.

messages.name: SpaceSeparatedSpecs
messages.documentation: Prefer `name [version [build]]` match spec syntax.

messages.name: OSVersion
messages.documentation: Prefer `name [version [build]]` match spec syntax.
messages.message: The feedstock is lowering the image versions for one or more platforms: ${platforms} (the default is ${default}). Unless you are in the very rare case of repackaging binary artifacts, consider removing these overrides from conda-forge.yml in the top feedstock directory.

messages.name: UsePip
messages.documentation:
Python packages should be built with `pip install ...`, not `python setup.py install`,
which is deprecated.
messages.message: Whenever possible python packages should use pip. See https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/adding_pkgs.html#use-pip

messages.name: UsePyPIOrg
messages.documentation:
Grayskull and the conda-forge example recipe used to have pypi.io as a default,
but the canonical URL is now PyPI.org.

See https://github.com/conda-forge/staged-recipes/pull/27946.
messages.message: PyPI default URL is now pypi.org, and not pypi.io. You may want to update the default source url.

messages.name: FormattedSelectors
messages.documentation:
Recipe format v0 (`meta.yaml`) supports the notion of line selectors
as trailing comments:

```yaml
build:
  skip: true  # [not win]
```

These must be formatted with two spaces before the `#` symbol, followed
by one space before the opening square bracket `[`, followed by no spaces.
The closing bracket must not be surrounded by spaces either.
messages.message: Selectors are suggested to take a ``<two spaces>#<one space>[<expression>]`` form. See lines ${lines}

messages.name: OldPythonSelectorsLint
messages.documentation:
Recipe v0 selectors used to include one Python version selector
per release, like `py27` for Python 2.7 and `py35` for Python 3.5.
This was deprecated in favor of the `py` integer, which is preferred.
messages.message: Old-style Python selectors (py27, py35, etc) are only available for Python 2.7, 3.4, 3.5, and 3.6. Please use explicit comparisons with the integer ``py``, e.g. ``# [py==37]`` or ``# [py>=37]``. See lines ${lines}

messages.name: OldPythonSelectorsHint
messages.documentation:
Recipe v0 selectors (see [`R0-002`](#r0-002)) used to include one Python
version selector per release, like `py27` for Python 2.7 and `py35` for Python 3.5.
This was deprecated in favor of the `py` integer, which is preferred.
messages.message: Old-style Python selectors (py27, py34, py35, py36) are deprecated. Instead, consider using the int ``py``. For example: ``# [py>=36]``. See lines ${lines}

messages.name: NoarchSelectorsV0
messages.documentation: Noarch packages are not generally compatible with v0 selectors
messages.message:
`noarch` packages can't have ${skips}selectors. If the selectors are necessary, please remove `noarch: ${noarch}`, or selector on line ${line_number}:
${line}

messages.name: JinjaDefinitions
messages.documentation: In v0 recipes, Jinja definitions must follow a particular style.
messages.message: Jinja2 variable definitions are suggested to take a ``{%<one space>set<one space><variable name><one space>=<one space><expression><one space>%}`` form. See lines ${lines}

messages.name: LegacyToolchain
messages.documentation:
The `toolchain` package is deprecated. Use compilers as outlined in
<https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#compilers>.
messages.message: Using toolchain directly in this manner is deprecated. Consider using the compilers outlined [conda-forge.org > Docs > Maintainer Documentation > Knowledge Base > Compilers](https://conda-forge.org/docs/maintainer/knowledge_base.html#compilers).

messages.name: NoCommentSelectors
messages.documentation: Recipe v0 selectors (see [`R0-002`](#r0-002)) are not supported in v1 recipes.
messages.message: Selectors in comment form no longer work in v1 recipes. Instead, if / then / else maps must be used. See lines ${lines}.

messages.name: NoarchSelectorsV1
messages.documentation: Noarch packages are not generally compatible with v1 conditional blocks.
messages.message: `noarch` packages can't have ${skips}selectors. If the selectors are necessary, please remove `noarch: ${noarch}`.

messages.name: RattlerBldBat
messages.documentation: `rattler-build` does not use `bld.bat` scripts, but `build.bat`.
messages.message: Found `bld.bat` in recipe directory, but this is a recipe v1 (rattler-build recipe). rattler-build uses `build.bat` instead of `bld.bat` for Windows builds. Consider renaming `bld.bat` to `build.bat`.

messages.name: MacOSDeploymentTargetRename
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: The `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET` key in ${recipe_config_file} needs to be removed or replaced by `c_stdlib_version`, appropriately restricted to macOS.

messages.name: MacOSDeploymentTargetBelow
messages.documentation: https://github.com/conda-forge/conda-forge.github.io/issues/2102
messages.message: You are setting `c_stdlib_version` on macOS below the current global baseline in conda-forge (${baseline_version}).

messages.name: MoreThanOneConfigFile
messages.documentation: Only one recipe variants file must be used in a feedstock.
messages.message: Found two recipe configuration files, but you may only use one! You may use `conda_build_config.yaml` for both v0 and v1 recipes, while `variants.yaml` may only be used with v1 recipes.
